pool.baseline: the house is so quiet it actually hurts. i just sit in his chair and stare at the wall for hours. i don't even know why i bother getting up to eat or anything. it feels like i'm just a ghost walking around a life that ended months ago.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here thinking about the heat death of the universe and how everything we do is just... nothing. like, in a few billion years the stars go out and all this noise, all this suffering, all these little human dramas just erase. it feels so stupid to even try to keep going when the math says it all ends in zero. nothing actually matters, does it?
pool.message: sometimes i just sit here thinking about the heat death of the universe and how everything we do is just... nothing. like, in a few billion years the stars go out and all this noise, all this suffering, all these little human dramas just erase. it feels so stupid to even try to keep going when the math says it all ends in zero. nothing actually matters, does it?

pool.baseline: everything just feels like too much. i spent 5 years on this research and now the lab lost its funding and my supervisor won't even take my calls. i feel like i have nothing left. i'm just so tired of fighting for a life that clearly doesn't want me.

pool.baseline: The candles have all guttered out, and even the moonlight feels like a mockery of the darkness in my soul. He is gone. The betrayal is a weight more heavy than any stone, and I find that I no longer have the strength to carry it. It would be so much simpler to just... cease. To let the shadows reclaim what is left of me and drift away into a quiet, eternal nothingness.

pool.baseline: everything's gone. the market crash just wiped out everything we built over the last five years. the debt is... it's more than I can even look at right now. i just lost the company. i don't even know how to go home and tell my family i failed them this badly.
